title: Pilosella pseudovahlii
taxonomy: Pilosella pseudovahlii is a member of the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of dandelion-like plants. The Pilosella genus is often considered a subset of the Taraxacum genus in some classifications. However, Pilosella pseudovahlii is generally recognized as a distinct species within the Pilosella genus. It is closely related to other species in the Pilosella genus.

intro: Pilosella pseudovahlii, a member of the dandelion family (Asteraceae), is a perennial plant that resembles a miniature dandelion in appearance. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella pseudovahlii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Pilosella pseudovahlii is often used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
